title: Method for device counting
abstract: UEs that connect to a wireless communication system within a specified time period are counted upon a first connection to the wireless communication system. To that end, a node in the network determines whether a connecting (or connected) UE was previously connected to any node for a vendor during a counting time window, where the counting time window is specified/defined for a group of vendors comprising one or more vendors serving the wireless communication system. If it is determined that the UE was not previously connected to any network nodes associated with the group of vendors, a device count is incremented. Otherwise, the device count is not incremented.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method (600) of counting user equipment (102) connected to a wireless communication system (100), the method (600) comprising: defining (602) a counting time window for a group of vendors comprising one or more vendors serving the wireless communication system (100), wherein the counting time window is bounded by a starting time and an ending time; determining (604) whether a connection of a user equipment (102) to a network node (106) for a vendor in the group of vendors is a first connection, during the counting time window, of the user equipment (102) to any of the network nodes (106) of the group of vendors; and in response to determining that the connection is the first connection during the counting time window, incrementing (606) a device count specific to the counting time window and to the group of vendors. 2. The method (600) of claim 1 wherein determining whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection comprises: obtaining a status of a not-yet-counted flag for the user equipment (102), wherein the not- yet-counted flag identifies whether the user equipment (102) has previously connected to any of the one or more network nodes (106) associated with the group of vendors during the counting time window; and determining whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection based on the status of the not-yet-counted flag. 3. The method (600) of claim 2 wherein: obtaining the status of the not-yet-counted flag comprises a mobility management entity (108) in the wireless network (100) obtaining the status of the not-yet-counted flag from memory (1020); and determining (604) whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection comprises the mobility management entity (108) determining whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection based on the determined status of the not-yet-counted flag. 4. The method (600) of claim 3 further comprising the mobility management entity (108) resetting the status of all not-yet-counted flags stored in the memory (1020) of the mobility management entity (108) at a starting time of a subsequent counting time window. 5. The method (600) of claim 3 further comprising the mobility management entity (108) changing the status of the not-yet-counted flag if the obtained status indicates that the connection is the first connection. 6. The method (600) of claim 1 wherein determining (604) whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection comprises: obtaining a timestamp for the connected user equipment (102), the timestamp representing a previous time the user equipment (102) connected to any network node (106) associated with the group of vendors; and determining whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection based on the relationship of the obtained timestamp to the counting time window. 7. The method (600) of claim 6 wherein obtaining the timestamp comprises the network node (106) obtaining the timestamp for the connected user equipment (102) from a mobility management entity (108) of the wireless communication system (100). 8. The method (600) of claim 6 wherein obtaining the timestamp comprises a mobility management entity (108) retrieving the timestamp from memory (1020). 9. The method (600) of any of claims 6-8 wherein determining (604) whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection based on the relationship of the determined timestamp to the counting time window comprises determining that the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection when the timestamp represents a time before the starting time of the counting time window. 10. The method (600) of any of claims 6-9 further comprising updating the timestamp responsive to the time of the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106). 1 1. The method (600) of claim 1 wherein determining whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection comprises: determining whether the user equipment (102) was already connected to the network node (106) at the starting time of the counting time window; and determining the connection is the first connection for the counting time window when the user equipment (102) is determined to have already been connected to the network node (106) at the starting time of the counting time window. 12. The method (600) of claim 1 wherein determining (604) whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection comprises determining whether a transition of the user equipment (102) from a first state to a connected state is the first connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106). 13. The method (600) of claim 12 wherein the transition of the user equipment (102) from the first state to the connected state comprises any one of: an establishment of a Radio Resource Control (RRC) connection for the user equipment (102); a re-establishment of an RRC connection for the user equipment (102); a resumption of an RRC connection for the user equipment (102); an implementation of a handover procedure; and an implementation of a first data transmission. 14. The method (600) of any of claims 1-13 further comprising obtaining, for the group of vendors, a total device count for the counting time window responsive to the device count determined by one or more of the network nodes (106) for the group of vendors. 15. The method (600) of claim 14 wherein obtaining the total device count comprises: a collection node (120) receiving the device count from each corresponding network node (106) for the group of vendors; and the collection node (120) summing the received device counts to determine, for the group of vendors, the total device count for the counting time window. 16. The method (600) of claim 15 wherein the collection node (120) receives the device count from each corresponding network node (106): after the ending time of the counting time window; responsive to a request sent from the collection node (120) to the corresponding network node (106); each time the device count is incremented; and/or after expiration of a timer. 17. The method (600) of claim 15 further comprising the collection node (120) resetting the total device count for the group of vendors at a starting time of a subsequent counting time window. 18. The method (600) of claim 14 wherein obtaining the total device count comprises: 

 a mobility management entity (108) receiving, for the group of vendors, the device count for the counting time window from each corresponding network node (106) for the group of vendors; and the mobility management entity (108) reporting each received device count to a collection node (120). 19. The method (600) of claim 18: further comprising the mobility management entity (108) summing the received device counts for the group of vendors to determine, for the group of vendors, the total device count for the counting time window; wherein the reporting, by the mobility management entity (108), of each received device count to the collection node (120) comprises the mobility management entity (108) reporting the total device count for the counting time window to the collection node (120). 20. A computer program product for controlling a node (106, 108, 1 10) in a wireless communication system (100), the computer program product comprising software instructions which, when run on at least one processing circuit (1000) in the node (106, 108, 1 10), causes the node (106, 108, 1 10) to execute the method (600) according to any one of claims 1-19. 21. A computer-readable medium comprising the computer program product of claim 20. 22. The computer-readable medium of claim 21 wherein the computer-readable medium comprises a non-transitory computer-readable medium. 23. A wireless communication system (100) comprising one or more nodes (106, 108, 1 10) configured to count a number of user equipment (102) connected to the wireless communication system (100), the one or more nodes (106, 108, 1 10) of the wireless communication system (100) configured to execute the method (600) according to any one of claims 1-19. 24. A network node (106) in a wireless communication system (100), the network node (106) comprising: a receiver circuit (1040) configured to obtain a counting time window for a group of vendors comprising one or more vendors, wherein at least one vendor in the group of vendors is associated with the network node (106), and wherein the counting time window is bounded by a starting time and an ending time; a processing circuit (1030) configured to: 

 determine whether a connection of a user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is a first connection, during the counting time window, of the user equipment (102) to any network node (106) in the wireless communication system (100) associated with the group of vendors; and in response to determining that the connection is the first connection during the counting time window, increment a device count for the counting time window and the group of vendors; and a transmitter circuit (1050) configured to report the device count to a remote node (108, 110, 120) in the wireless communication system (100). 25. The network node (106) of claim 24 wherein: the receiver circuit (1040) is further configured to receive a timestamp for the connected user equipment (102) from a mobility management entity (108), the received timestamp representing a previous time the user equipment (102) connected to any network node (106) associated with the group of vendors; and the processing circuit (1030) determines whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection based on the relationship of the received timestamp to the counting time window. 26. The network node (106) of claim 25 wherein the processing circuit (1030) determines whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection based on the relationship of the determined timestamp to the counting time window by determining that the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection when the timestamp represents a time before the starting time of the counting time window. 27. The network node (106) of claim 24 wherein the processing circuit (1030) determines whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection by: determining whether the user equipment (102) was already connected to the network node (106) at the starting time of the counting time window; and determining the connection is the first connection for the counting time window when the user equipment (102) is determined to have already been connected to the network node (106) at the starting time of the counting time window. 28. The network node (106) of claim 24 wherein the processing circuit (1030) determines whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first 

 connection by determining whether a transition of the user equipment (102) from a first state to a connected state is the first connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106). 29. The network node (106) of claim 28 wherein the transition of the user equipment (102) from the first state to the connected state comprises any one of: an establishment of a Radio Resource Control (RRC) connection for the user equipment (102); a re-establishment of an RRC connection for the user equipment (102); a resumption of an RRC connection for the user equipment (102); an implementation of a handover procedure; and an implementation of a first data transmission. 30. The network node (106) of any of claims 24-29 wherein the transmitter circuit (1050) is configured to report the device count to a mobility management entity (108) and/or a collection node (120) in the wireless communication system (100). 31. The network node (106) of claim 30 wherein the transmitter circuit (1050) reports the device count: after the ending time of the counting time window; responsive to a request received from the collection node (120); each time the processing circuit (1030) increments the vendor count; and/or after expiration of a timer in the network node (106). 32. A mobility management entity (108) for a plurality of network nodes (106) in a wireless communication system (100), wherein one or more of the network nodes (106) are associated with a group of vendors comprising one or more vendors, the mobility management entity (108) comprising: a receiver circuit (1040) configured to obtain a counting time window for the group of vendors, wherein the counting time window is bounded by a starting time and an ending time; and a processing circuit (1030) configured to: determine whether a connection of a user equipment (102) to one of the or more network nodes (106) associated with the group of vendors is a first connection, during the counting time window, of the user equipment (102) to any of the one or more network nodes (106) associated with the group of vendors; and in response to determining that the connection is the first connection during the counting time window, increment a device count for the counting time window. 33. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 32 wherein the processing circuit (1030) determines whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection by: retrieving, from memory (1010), a not-yet-counted flag for the user equipment (102), wherein the not-yet-counted flag identifies whether the user equipment (102) has previously connected to any of the one or more network nodes (106) associated with the group of vendors during the counting time window; and determining whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection based on a status of the retrieved not-yet-counted flag. 34. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 33 wherein the processing circuit (1030) is further configured to reset all not-yet-counted flags stored in the memory (1020) at the starting time of a subsequent counting time window. 35. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 33 wherein the processing circuit (1030) is further configured to: change the status of the not-yet-counted flag if the retrieved not-yet-counted flag indicates that the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection to generate an updated not-yet-counted flag; and store the updated not-yet-counted flag in memory. 36. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 32 wherein the processing circuit (1030) determines whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection by: retrieving, from memory (1020), a timestamp for the connected user equipment (102), the timestamp representing a previous time the user equipment (102) connected to any network node (106) associated with the group of vendors; and determining whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection based on the relationship of the obtained timestamp to the counting time window. 37. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 36 wherein the processing circuit (1030) determines whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection based on the relationship of the determined timestamp to the counting time window by determining that the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection when the timestamp represents a time before the starting time of the counting time window. 38. The mobility management entity (108) of any of claims 36-37 wherein the processing circuit (1030) is further configured to update the timestamp responsive to a time of the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106). 39. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 32 wherein the processing circuit (1030) determines whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection by: determining whether the user equipment (102) was already connected to the network node (106) at the starting time of the counting time window; and determining the connection is the first connection when the user equipment (102) is determined to have already been connected to the network node (106) at the starting time of the counting time window. 40. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 32 wherein the processing circuit (1030) determines whether the connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106) is the first connection by determining whether a transition of the user equipment (102) from a first state to a connected state is the first connection of the user equipment (102) to the network node (106). 41. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 40 wherein the transition of the user equipment (102) from the first state to the connected state comprises any one of: an establishment of a Radio Resource Control (RRC) connection for the user equipment (102); a re-establishment of an RRC connection for the user equipment (102); a resumption of an RRC connection for the user equipment (102); an implementation of a handover procedure; and an implementation of a first data transmission. 42. The mobility management entity (108) of any of claims 32-41 wherein the processing circuit (1030) is further configured to determine, for the group of vendors, a total device count for the counting time window responsive to the device count determined by the processing circuit (1030) and/or one or more device counts received from one or more of the network nodes (106) for the group of vendors. 43. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 42 wherein the processing circuit (1030) is further configured to reset the total device count for the group of vendors at a starting time of a subsequent counting time window. 44. The mobility management entity (108) of any of claims 32-43 further comprising a transmitter circuit (1050) configured to send the device count to a collection node (120). 45. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 44 wherein: the receiver circuit (1040) is further configured to receive, for the group of vendors, an additional device count for the counting time window from one or more additional network nodes (106) associated with the group of vendors; and the transmitter circuit (1050) sends the device count to the collection node (120) by sending each device count to the collection node (120). 46. The mobility management entity (108) of claim 45 wherein: the processing circuit (1030) is further configured to sum the device count with each of the additional device counts received for the group of vendors to determine, for the group of vendors, the total device count for the counting time window; and the transmitter circuit (1050) sends each device count to the collection node (120) by sending the total device count for the counting time window to the collection node (120) .
